posts.title: Building Height Measurement
posts.text: Arduino project in experimental physics to determine the height of a building based on the acceleration measured in an elevator using the MPU6050 sensor. Data analysis performed in Python using Pandas, NumPy, and Matplotlib. I will describe in more details later. Meanwhile you can check the project on this Github Link

posts.title: Blockchain Applied to an Electoral Process
posts.text: Implementation of a blockchain applied to an electoral process in C language. More detailed description will be provided later.

posts.title: Efficiency of FFT based polynom multiplicaton
posts.text: Implementation and analysis of the efficiency of the FFT-based algorithm for multiplying two polynomials. I will describe in more details later. Meanwhile you can check the project on this Github Link

posts.title: Heatsink
posts.text: Simulatation of a heatsink using a 3D model. It is intended for educational purposes to demonstrate the principles of computing heat transfer using parallel algorithms and meshing data across multiple dimensions. More detailed description will be provided later.

posts.title: FFT Parallelization
posts.text: Parallelization of an FFT-based algorithm that generates a ”damped” sound, reminiscent of what one might hear underwater. I will describe in more details later. Meanwhile you can check the project on this Github Link

posts.title: Motif Search in Genome
posts.text: Application of motif search algorithms in genomes in bioinformatics, using Deep Learning with Python. The genomic sequences were in FASTA format. More detailed description will be provided later.

posts.title: Network Traffic Visualizer
posts.text: Development of network traffic visualization software in C, including the analysis of frames exchanged between machines identified by MAC addresses, IPs, and port numbers. The software supports IPv4, UDP, TCP, HTTP, and ICMP protocols. I will describe in more details later. Meanwhile you can check the project on this Github Link

posts.title: Pseudospectra Calculation
posts.text: Study, implementation, and parallelization of different algorithms to calculate the pseudospectrum of a matrix. Development of an interface allowing a user to manipulate the analysis of the pseudospectrum of a matrix. I will describe in more details later. Meanwhile you can check the project on this Github Link

posts.title: Vertex Cover
posts.text: Implementation of an undirected graph and analysis of different algorithms to solve the Vertex Cover problem. I will describe in more details later. Meanwhile you can check the project on this Github Link

posts.title: Full-stack Web Project
posts.text: Design and development of a social network website similar to Twitter using React. More detailed description will be provided later.

pages.title: Welcome to my website
pages.text: Who am IMy name is Lasha Gogritchiani. In December 2025, I graduated with a Master of Computer Science, specifically the Cryptology, High-performance computing and Algorithms curriculum at Sorbonne University, which I completed on the “Excellency track.” Before that, I finished my Bachelor of Computer Science with a minor in Physics, also at Sorbonne University. I am particularly interested in post-quantum and quantum cryptography, implementation security, and the efficiency of primitives. Currently, I am looking for a PhD or a full-time position at the intersection of at least two of these interests. About this siteI decided to build this website mainly to discuss and gather my personal and professional projects. In my free time, I will also try to share and discuss aspects of cryptography and related topics in the form of short blog posts. The topics discussed may vary in difficulty; I will cover basics for quick reading to sensitize the general public to fundamental aspects of cryptography, as well as more advanced topics. Currently, the main sections of the website are organized as follows: CV: This section describes my educational and professional background, my certifications, and my volunteering activities. PROJECTS: A section dedicated to describe my projects. BLOG: Discussions on various topics, generally kept in a short-reading format. The rest of the site is mainly used for navigation. This website will be written primarily in English, but in some cases, I might also write blogs in French or Georgian. I will use dedicated tags for those languages.
